q: In libbpf 1.0, create a BPF hash map directly from userspace (not from a BTF-defined SEC(".maps") object). Give the single low-level function call and its OPTS argument.
fact: The six legacy bpf_create_map*() functions are removed; the only userspace map-creation call is bpf_map_create() (OPTS-based).
note: Stock model reaches for bpf_create_map()/bpf_create_map_xattr() from pre-1.0 libbpf; those symbols no longer exist.

q: In libbpf 1.0, load a BPF program into the kernel using the low-level (bpf syscall wrapper) API, passing instructions and log buffer via OPTS. Name the function.
fact: bpf_load_program(), bpf_load_program_xattr() and bpf_verify_program() are gone; use bpf_prog_load().
note: Base emits bpf_load_program_xattr(), the classic pre-1.0 loader.

q: Write the BPF-side (kernel) definition of a hash map keyed by u32 to u64 for a CO-RE libbpf 1.0 program. Give the exact section annotation the map struct lives in.
fact: Legacy SEC("maps") + struct bpf_map_def is dropped in v1.0; only BTF-defined maps in SEC(".maps") load.
note: Stock model writes SEC("maps") with struct bpf_map_def; libbpf 1.0 silently refuses to load it.

q: Given a struct bpf_program *prog in libbpf 1.0, get its program type with the accessor function. Give the call.
fact: Getters dropped the get_ prefix: bpf_program__get_type() -> bpf_program__type() (setters keep set_).
note: Base uses bpf_program__get_type(), the pre-1.0 getter name.

q: In libbpf 1.0 (strict mode is default), you call a low-level API such as bpf_map_update_elem(). Show the correct way to test its integer return value for failure and obtain the error code.
fact: int-returning APIs now return -Exxx directly; check err < 0, not err == -1.
note: Base writes `if (ret == -1)` with errno; under strict-direct-errs that comparison silently never matches.

q: In libbpf 1.0, you call bpf_object__open_file(). Show how to detect that it failed and retrieve the error code.
fact: Constructor APIs now return NULL on error (errno set); libbpf_get_error()/ERR_PTR handling is removed.
note: Base checks libbpf_get_error(obj) / IS_ERR(obj); with clean-ptrs the pointer is plain NULL and that path is wrong.

q: In libbpf 1.0, find a specific BPF program in a bpf_object by its ELF section name. The old single-call helper is gone; give the idiomatic replacement.
fact: bpf_object__find_program_by_title() removed; iterate with bpf_object__for_each_program() and compare bpf_program__section_name().
note: Base calls bpf_object__find_program_by_title(), which no longer exists.

q: In libbpf 1.0, retrieve the ELF section name of a struct bpf_program *. Give the accessor call.
fact: bpf_program__title() renamed to bpf_program__section_name().
note: Base uses bpf_program__title() (the pre-1.0 name).

q: In libbpf 1.0, iterate over all maps in a bpf_object by hand (get the next map given the current one). Give the function.
fact: bpf_map__next() renamed to bpf_object__next_map() (it is a method of bpf_object).
note: Base uses bpf_map__next(map, obj); that symbol is gone.

q: In libbpf 1.0, open a BPF object from an ELF file on disk with optional open-opts. Give the function you pass the file path to.
fact: bpf_object__open()/open_buffer()/open_xattr() removed; use bpf_object__open_file() and bpf_object__open_mem().
note: Base calls bpf_object__open(path) or bpf_object__open_xattr().

q: In libbpf 1.0, change a map's max_entries before load from userspace. Give the setter call.
fact: bpf_map__resize() replaced by bpf_map__set_max_entries().
note: Base uses bpf_map__resize(); it is removed.

q: In libbpf 1.0, you are done with a loaded bpf_object and want to unload it from the kernel and free all resources. Give the single call.
fact: bpf_object__unload() removed; bpf_object__close() unloads and frees (objects are not re-loadable).
note: Base pairs bpf_object__unload() with a later close; unload no longer exists.

q: Write the SEC() annotation for a TC (traffic control / cls_bpf) classifier BPF program in libbpf 1.0.
fact: SEC("classifier")/SEC("action") are legacy; use SEC("tc") or SEC("tc/ingress"|"tc/egress").
note: Base writes SEC("classifier"), the classic BCC/old-libbpf tc annotation.

q: In a libbpf 1.0 skeleton, read a zero-initialized (uninitialized) global variable named counter from userspace. Which skeleton member holds it?
fact: .bss is merged into .data; access via skel->data->counter, and skel->bss is being removed.
note: Base reads skel->bss->counter; that pointer is gone / NULL in 1.0 skeletons.

q: In libbpf 1.0, upload an in-memory struct btf * into the kernel. Give the function.
fact: btf__load() renamed to btf__load_into_kernel().
note: Base calls btf__load(btf); ambiguous old name is deprecated/removed.

q: In libbpf 1.0, run a loaded BPF program in test mode with input/output buffers passed via an OPTS struct. Give the function.
fact: bpf_prog_test_run_xattr()/bpf_prog_test_run() removed; use bpf_prog_test_run_opts().
note: Base uses bpf_prog_test_run_xattr(); it no longer exists.

q: You ported a tool from BCC to libbpf. On the very first map/program load your userspace program fails with a memlock/ENOMEM style error, though the identical logic worked under BCC. What must the libbpf userspace code do that BCC did for you?
fact: BCC unconditionally set RLIMIT_MEMLOCK to infinity; libbpf does not, so you must raise it yourself.
note: Base assumes locked-memory is handled automatically (BCC behavior) and never raises RLIMIT_MEMLOCK.

q: In a libbpf CO-RE program, read a nested kernel struct field: the parent task's pid, given struct task_struct *tsk (i.e. tsk->parent->pid). Show the correct, verifier-safe access.
fact: BCC silently rewrote tsk->parent->pid into bpf_probe_read calls; libbpf CO-RE requires BPF_CORE_READ(tsk, parent, pid).
note: Base writes the direct chain tsk->parent->pid, which fails to verify / reads garbage under CO-RE.

q: In a libbpf BPF program, send an event payload to userspace through a perf event array map named events (BCC's events.perf_submit(ctx, &data, sizeof(data)) equivalent). Show the call.
fact: BCC's events.perf_submit(...) becomes bpf_perf_event_output(ctx, &events, BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU, &data, sizeof(data)).
note: Base emits the BCC method events.perf_submit(); libbpf has no such method.

q: Migrate this BCC map declaration to libbpf: BPF_HASH(counts, u32, u64). Give the libbpf-side declaration.
fact: BCC's BPF_HASH macro becomes an explicit struct with __uint(type, BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH) in SEC(".maps").
note: Base keeps the BCC BPF_HASH() macro, which does not exist in libbpf.

q: Porting a BCC BPF_HASH to a libbpf SEC(".maps") struct, you specify type/key/value but leave the size off. Why does the map fail, and what field must you add?
fact: BCC defaulted map size to 10240; libbpf requires an explicit __uint(max_entries, N).
note: Base assumes the implicit 10240 default from BCC and omits max_entries, so the map has 0 entries / fails.

q: In a libbpf BPF program, look up a key in a hash map named counts (BCC's counts.lookup(&key)). Show the call that returns the value pointer.
fact: BCC's map.lookup(&key) becomes bpf_map_lookup_elem(&map, &key).
note: Base uses the BCC pseudo-method counts.lookup(&key), invalid in libbpf.

q: In a modern libbpf BPF program, print a debug line with one integer argument. Give the helper macro/call.
fact: bpf_trace_printk() is replaced by bpf_printk() (max 3 args).
note: Base uses bpf_trace_printk() with the old manual fmt-length signature.

q: Attach a kprobe to the kill(2) syscall entry on a modern x86-64 Linux kernel (5.x) using a libbpf SEC() annotation. Give the exact section string.
fact: Since kernel 4.17 the x64 syscall entry is __x64_sys_kill, not sys_kill; attaching to sys_kill silently finds nothing.
note: Base writes SEC("kprobe/sys_kill"); the kprobe never fires on 4.17+ x64.

q: Pass a compile-time-immutable configuration constant (e.g. a target PID filter) into a libbpf CO-RE BPF program so userspace can set it before load. Show the BPF-side variable declaration.
fact: BCC's #define config substitution is replaced by a global `const volatile` variable (const volatile prevents the compiler zero-inlining it).
note: Base uses #define TARGET_PID or a plain global; without const volatile the compiler inlines a zero and the filter breaks.

q: What single header must a libbpf CO-RE BPF program #include to get all kernel type definitions (instead of BCC's implicit <linux/...> includes)?
fact: BCC's implicit #include <linux/...> is replaced by a single #include "vmlinux.h".
note: Base includes <linux/sched.h>, <uapi/linux/ptrace.h>, etc. as under BCC; CO-RE programs use vmlinux.h.

q: Attach a loaded XDP program (by fd) to a network interface index using the current libbpf/libxdp low-level helper. Give the function.
fact: bpf_set_link_xdp_fd() replaced by bpf_xdp_attach().
note: Base calls bpf_set_link_xdp_fd(ifindex, fd, ...), which is removed.

q: Query the BPF program id currently attached in XDP mode on an interface using the current libbpf API. Give the function.
fact: bpf_get_link_xdp_id() replaced by bpf_xdp_query_id().
note: Base uses bpf_get_link_xdp_id(); removed.

q: Retrieve full XDP attachment info (modes/prog ids) for an interface with the current libbpf API. Give the function.
fact: bpf_get_link_xdp_info() replaced by bpf_xdp_query().
note: Base uses bpf_get_link_xdp_info(); removed.

q: You are writing an AF_XDP (XSK) userspace app using xsk_socket__create / xsk_umem__create. With libbpf 1.0, which library must you include the xsk.h header from and link against now?
fact: AF_XDP xsk.h APIs moved out of libbpf into libxdp; libbpf 1.0 dropped them.
note: Base tells you to link libbpf and #include <bpf/xsk.h>; that header/API is gone from libbpf 1.0.

q: In Cilium 1.15 Helm/agent config, fully replace kube-proxy. Give the exact value for the kube-proxy-replacement setting.
fact: kube-proxy-replacement values strict/partial/disabled were removed in v1.15; only true/false are valid.
note: Base sets kube-proxy-replacement: strict (the pre-1.15 value); the agent rejects it.

q: In Cilium 1.15 Helm values, run in native-routing mode with no overlay/encapsulation. Give the value(s) you set (the old `tunnel: disabled` was removed).
fact: The tunnel option was removed; use routingMode=native (was tunnel=disabled) / tunnelProtocol for the protocol.
note: Base sets tunnel: disabled or tunnel=disabled; that key no longer exists in 1.15.

q: Write the apiVersion line for a CiliumLoadBalancerIPPool manifest on Cilium 1.15+.
fact: CiliumLoadBalancerIPPool v2alpha1 was deprecated/removed; use apiVersion cilium.io/v2.
note: Base writes apiVersion: cilium.io/v2alpha1, the older API version.

q: In a CiliumNetworkPolicy egress rule, require mutual authentication for the connection. Give the exact YAML attribute and a valid value.
fact: The rule attribute auth.type was renamed to authentication.mode (values disabled/required/test-always-fail).
note: Base writes auth: {type: required}; the field is silently ignored under the new schema.

q: On a node running Cilium 1.15, what is the exact filename of the CNI configuration file Cilium writes into /etc/cni/net.d?
fact: Cilium now writes 05-cilium.conflist instead of 05-cilium.conf.
note: Base answers 05-cilium.conf (old name); note substring overlap prevents an avoid_old token, so grade the conflist suffix.

q: On Cilium 1.15+ you want to configure BGP peering to a ToR. The old CiliumBGPPeeringPolicy CRD is gone. Which CRD generation must you use?
fact: CiliumBGPPeeringPolicy CRD and the BGPv1 control plane were removed; migrate to the v2 BGP CRDs.
note: Base writes a CiliumBGPPeeringPolicy manifest (BGPv1), which no longer exists. UNSURE: facts name only 'v2 CRDs', not the exact new CRD names, so grade loosely.

q: On FRR 10.5+, a BGP UPDATE arrives whose AS_PATH contains an AS_SET segment. With default configuration, what does FRR do with that route?
fact: Per RFC 9774, FRR 10.5 rejects AS_SET by default (previously accepted).
note: Base says the route is accepted (pre-10.5 default); grading is knowledge-style. UNSURE: 'accept' is a substring of 'not accept' so no avoid_old token.

q: On FRR 10.5 with the datacenter profile, does a BGP session advertise the IPv6 Link-Local Next Hop capability by default?
fact: IPv6 Link-Local capability was enabled by default for the datacenter profile in 10.4.0 but is disabled by default from 10.5.0.
note: Base says it is enabled by default for datacenter (the 10.4 behavior). Knowledge-style grading.

q: On FRR 10 (integrated-config setup), an operator edits BGP config then runs 'write memory'. Which file now persists the change, and does bgpd.conf get updated?
fact: 'write memory' no longer updates per-daemon files (bgpd.conf etc.); it writes the integrated /etc/frr/frr.conf.
note: Base says write memory rewrites the per-daemon bgpd.conf; that no longer happens, so edits appear lost.

q: You configure PIM multicast on FRR 10 and it fails to operate though the config looks like older releases. What newly-required configuration is now mandatory that used to be optional?
fact: pimd/pim6d now require router alert configuration; without it PIM fails to operate.
note: Base omits router-alert (it was optional pre-10), so the PIM setup silently breaks.

q: On FRR 10, configure a VRF/network namespace binding in zebra using the old 'netns' command. Is that command still available?
fact: The deprecated netns command was dropped from zebra.
note: Base offers the zebra 'netns NAME' command; it has been removed. Knowledge-style grading.

q: In DPDK 24.x (24.11), set the name of a thread you created. Give the function.
fact: rte_thread_setname() replaced by rte_thread_set_name().
note: Base calls rte_thread_setname(); removed.

q: In DPDK 24.11, exchange packets with the Linux kernel networking stack using the KNI (Kernel Network Interface) library (rte_kni_*). Is KNI still available?
fact: The KNI library and driver were removed in DPDK 24.
note: Base writes rte_kni_init()/rte_kni_alloc() code; the whole KNI library is gone.

q: In DPDK 24.11, configure a port's RSS with a custom 52-byte hash key via rte_eth_rss_conf. Besides setting rss_key, what field must you now also set?
fact: rss_key_len is now required whenever rss_key != NULL; it no longer defaults to 40 bytes.
note: Base sets only rss_key and relies on the removed implicit 40-byte default, so the key is misread.

q: In DPDK 24.11, compare an ICMP packet's type against the echo-request constant. Give the macro name.
fact: RTE_IP_ICMP_ECHO_REQUEST renamed to RTE_ICMP_TYPE_ECHO_REQUEST.
note: Base uses RTE_IP_ICMP_ECHO_REQUEST, the old symbol.

q: Create an RDMA (RoCE) interface via the VPP 25.02 binary API. Give the current message name.
fact: rdma_create_v3 is deprecated; rdma_create_v4 is the current variant.
note: Base emits rdma_create_v3 (or v2), superseded by v4.

q: Add a GTP-U tunnel via the VPP 25.02 binary API. Give the current message name to use.
fact: gtpu_add_del_tunnel is superseded by gtpu_add_del_tunnel_v2.
note: Base uses gtpu_add_del_tunnel (v1); note the v1 name is a substring of the v2 name so no avoid_old token is possible.
